UNF+17.34% UAC+16.40% NATL+13.36% CWAN+12.88% KDP+10.40% IMXI+4.58% OGN+4.32% HPE+3.21% AES+2.95% WBD+2.44% SKYT+2.19% SLNO-0.02% JHG-5.41% KW-6.82% UNF+17.34% UAC+16.40% NATL+13.36% CWAN+12.88% KDP+10.40% IMXI+4.58% OGN+4.32% HPE+3.21% AES+2.95% WBD+2.44% SKYT+2.19% SLNO-0.02% JHG-5.41% KW-6.82%

MERIDIAN

◈ Live Deal Intelligence ◈

Institutional-grade merger arbitrage intelligence, sourced directly from SEC EDGAR. Every active deal tracked, scored, and updated hourly so you can act with conviction.

Accuracy
1hr

SEC
Source
◈ Proprietary Scoring Model
88.2% accuracy backtested on 51 historical deals from 2023–2025. Read the full methodology.
→
The Scoring System
◈ How Meridian evaluates every deal
80 to 100
Very Low Risk
High confidence deals. Strong buyer, clean regulatory path, healthy break fee. These are positions institutional arb funds hold with conviction.
65 to 79
Low Risk
Solid deals with minor uncertainties. Slightly longer timelines or modest regulatory complexity. Still strong risk-adjusted returns.
50 to 64
Medium Risk
Meaningful uncertainties including financing risk, antitrust scrutiny, or unconventional deal structures.
0 to 49
High Risk
Elevated break probability. Negative spreads, weak buyers, or significant regulatory headwinds. Approach with caution.
01
SEC EDGAR Monitoring
Every hour, Meridian scans 8-K filings for definitive merger agreements, automatically extracting deal price, acquirer, and terms.
02
Live Spread Calculation
Current market prices pulled in real time. The spread is the gap between market price and deal price — that gap is your potential return if the deal closes.
03
Proprietary Scoring
Our model scores each position on spread quality, deal type, and time pending. Backtested at 88.2% accuracy on 51 historical deals from 2023–2025.
Meridian · Built by Kaushal Koduru

Meridian · Built by Kaushal Koduru
Data · SEC EDGAR / Yahoo Finance
The Scoring Model
◈ Methodology & Backtest Results

Meridian's proprietary scoring model evaluates every pending M&A deal across three evidence-based factors. Each factor is weighted based on its historical predictive power across 51 closed and broken deals from 2023 to 2025.

88.2%
Overall Accuracy
Across all 51 deals
51
Deals Backtested
2023 through 2025
89.2%
Closed Deal Accuracy
33 of 37 correctly predicted
85.7%
Broken Deal Accuracy
12 of 14 correctly predicted
01
Spread Quality
Primary Factor · Highest Predictive Weight

The merger arbitrage spread is the single most powerful predictor of deal outcome. A tight spread means the market has high confidence the deal closes. A wide spread means the market is pricing in meaningful break risk.

Spread RangeMarket SignalScore Adjustment
0% to 3%Market fully confident · deal expected to close+25 points
3% to 5%Healthy spread · normal deal uncertainty+18 points
5% to 8%Moderate spread · some market concern+10 points
8% to 12%Wide spread · notable market skepticism+0 points
12% to 18%Very wide · serious concern about completion-15 points
18% to 25%Extreme spread · market pricing in high break risk-25 points
25%+Market pricing in near-certain deal break-35 points
NegativeMarket says deal has already effectively broken-25 points
02
Deal Structure
Secondary Factor · Deal Type Completion Rates

Different deal structures have meaningfully different completion rates. All-cash deals are the most reliable — there is no stock price risk and the acquirer has secured financing upfront.

Deal TypeCharacteristicsScore Adjustment
All CashHighest certainty · no stock price risk · financing secured+10 points
Tender OfferDirect shareholder approach · fast timeline · high completion rate+8 points
Private EquityFinancing dependent · leveraged · slightly more break risk+5 points
Cash + StockStock component adds market risk · more complex closing conditions+0 points
03
Time Pending
Tertiary Factor · Deal Duration Signal

Deals that have been pending longer face greater accumulated risk. However, this factor is weighted less aggressively because some legitimate deals take 18-24 months while still closing successfully.

Days PendingSignalScore Adjustment
Under 90 daysFresh deal · full regulatory timeline ahead · low accumulated risk+10 points
90 to 270 daysNormal progression · expected timeline for most deals+0 points
270 to 500 daysExtended timeline · possible complications-5 points
500+ daysVery extended · serious regulatory or financing concerns-15 points
04
Risk Classification
How Scores Map to Risk Levels · Base Score 70

All deals start at a base score of 70. The three factors adjust this score up or down. Scores at or above 70 correctly predicted deal closure in 89.2% of historical cases.

80–100
Very Low Risk
Market has high conviction. Tight spread, strong deal structure.
65–79
Low Risk
Solid completion probability. Minor uncertainties priced in.
50–64
Medium Risk
Elevated uncertainty. Wider spread reflects meaningful concern.
0–49
High Risk
Market pricing in significant break probability.
05
Sample Backtest Results
Selected deals from the 51-deal backtest · 2023–2025
TickerSpread at Ann.ScorePredictedActualCorrect
CACC4.0%98ClosedClosed✓
GTES3.0%98ClosedClosed✓
SGEN5.0%85ClosedClosed✓
SAVE35.0%30BrokenBroken✓
SGFY12.0%60BrokenBroken✓
CHNG22.0%40BrokenBroken✓
ATVI25.0%30BrokenClosed✗
ACI15.0%50BrokenClosed✗

The two notable misses — ATVI and ACI — were large acquisitions that faced prolonged antitrust battles but ultimately closed. Their wide spreads correctly signaled elevated risk, but the model classified them as breaks. This illustrates the model's primary limitation: it cannot distinguish between deals that are genuinely breaking and deals facing regulatory headwinds that eventually close.

Meridian · Built by Kaushal Koduru
Backtested · 51 historical deals · 2023–2025
Model v4 · May 2026
